title: Emergence of dual axion response in condensed matter
authors: Elina Kokurina, Dmitry Vagin, Eduardo Barredo-Alamilla, Maxim A. Gorlach
abstract: Recently, it was predicted that nonreciprocal magneto-electric effect in antiferromagnetic multilayered metamaterials occurs in two distinct versions. One is the conventional axion response, while another one is dual axion response captured by electrodynamics with magnetic charge. Here we investigate a model condensed matter system of spins coupled through antiferromagnetic exchange interaction and derive its effective electromagnetic properties. We predict that this system gives rise to the emergent dual axion field, support our conclusion by numerical simulations and put forward candidate materials.
summary: This paper investigates the emergence of a 'dual axion response' in condensed matter systems, specifically antiferromagnetic spin lattices. While previously predicted for metamaterials, the authors demonstrate that realistic materials like Cr2O3 can host this effect. They show that while standard scattering experiments cannot distinguish between conventional and dual axion responses, internal excitation probes can. This work proposes a new way to identify nonreciprocal magneto-electric coupling in solids.
main_problem: Investigating whether the recently predicted dual axion response, a nonreciprocal magneto-electric effect involving magnetic charge, can emerge in condensed matter systems like antiferromagnets rather than just in engineered metamaterials.
main_result: The authors prove that a periodic antiferromagnetic spin lattice exhibits an emergent dual axion response, which can be distinguished from the conventional axion response by analyzing the frequency dependence of polarization rotation from an internal current source.
method: The study uses linear response theory, the semi-classical Landau-Lifshitz-Gilbert (LLG) equation, and the Discrete Dipole Approximation (DDA) for numerical validation, alongside an effective medium approach.
model_or_system: A periodic antiferromagnetic spin lattice (e.g., Cr2O3 or MnBi2Te4) with Heisenberg-type exchange coupling, magnetic anisotropy, and dipole-dipole interactions.
key_observables: Axion coefficients (chi and dual axion chi-tilde), effective permeability, reflection and transmission coefficients (r_xy, t_xy), and polarization rotation (Ey/Ex).
important_parameters: Lattice period-to-wavelength ratio (xi), magnon resonance frequency, exchange constants (J, J_perp), and anisotropy (K).
main_assumptions: Effective medium limit (subwavelength regime), linearized spin dynamics, neglect of spatial dispersion, and neglect of transverse exchange coupling.
figures_summary: Figure 1 compares metamaterials to spin lattices; Figure 2/3 show the lattice geometry and calculated material parameters for Cr2O3; Figure 4/5/6/7 show reflection/transmission properties and the diagnostic comparison between axion and dual axion models using simulated current sources.
paper_structure: The paper establishes a hierarchy from metamaterials to spin lattices, derives effective electromagnetic properties from the LLG equation, validates the dual axion emergence via numerical simulations, and provides a diagnostic recipe for experimental identification.
why_interesting: The discovery of a dual axion response in natural solids suggests a new topological phase or invariant in condensed matter and provides a method to probe broken symmetries using internal radiation sources.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Hierarchy of the models featuring nonreciprocal magneto-electric effect. (a) Multilayered metamaterial com- posed of gyrotropic layers with out-of-plane magnetization features axion [22] or dual axion [31] response depending on the properties of the layers. (b) Our simplified model of the spin lattice with antiferromagnetic exchange coupling between the spins in the neighboring layers. (c) Realistic condensed matter structure Cr2O3 featuring an antiferromag- netic arrangement of Cr spins [32] and promising for observing dual axion response.
FIG. 2. Geometry of antiferromagnetic spin lattice and choice of the material boundary in the effective medium description.

FIG. 3. Calculated material parameters of Cr2O3 at fre- quencies close to magnon resonance. The parameters are a = 1.359 nm, a⊥ = 0.496 nm, s = 3/2, g = 2.0, ℏKeff/(2π) = 21.9 GHz, ℏJ/(2π) = 58.1 GHz [40] and α = 2.2 · 10−4 [41]. Magnon resonance frequency is 165 GHz. (a) Permeability. (b) Dual axion response. Solid and dashed lines indicate real and imaginary parts, respectively.

FIG. 4. Reflection and transmission properties of a finite slab of model antiferromagnetic structure resembling Cr2O3 calculated in the effective medium approximation. The total number of periods N = 2.0 · 107, permittivity ε = 1. (a) Co- polarized reflection. (b) Cross-polarized reflection. (c) Co- polarized transmission. Cross-polarized transmission vanishes identically.

FIG. 5. Reflection and transmission properties of a finite slab of model antiferromagnetic structure resembling Cr2O3 calculated via effective medium model (solid blue lines) and discrete dipole method (solid red lines). The total number of periods is N = 800, while the remaining parameters are the same as in Fig. 4.

FIG. 6. Probing the type of nonreciprocal magneto-electric effect by the external sources. (a) Geometry of excitation of antiferromagnetic spin lattice. (b,c) Co- and cross-polarized components of magnetic field produced by the planar cur- rent source. The magnetic field is presented in the arbitrary units. H0 denotes the magnetic field of a planar current in vacuum. Green and blue dashed lines correspond to the effec- tive medium calculation for axion and dual axion responses, respectively. Parameters of the simulation match those in Fig. 4.

FIG. 7. Cross-polarized component of magnetic field created by the current exciting our model antiferromagnetic structure consisting of N = 800 periods. Solid red line shows the re- sult of the discrete dipole model. Predictions of the effective medium model for the materials with axion or dual axion re- sponse are shown by the blue dashed and green solid lines, respectively. Parameters of the simulation match those in Fig. 5, magnetic field is shown in the arbitrary units having the same scale in all three cases.
